name: Molting Process
description: Molting Process in Arthropods. This process represents a higher-level biological mechanism distinct from molecular/biochemical processes covered in GLMP.
mermaid:
graph TD
    A10[Growth Signal] --> B10[Ecdysone Production]
    B10 --> C10[Apolysis Initiation]
    C10 --> D10[New Cuticle Secretion]
    D10 --> E10[Enzyme Production]
    E10 --> F10[Old Cuticle Digestion]
    F10 --> G10[Water Absorption]
    G10 --> H10[Cuticle Shedding]
    H10 --> I10[Sclerotization]
    I10 --> J10[New Exoskeleton]
    
    style A10 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style B10 fill:#ffd43b,color:#000
    style C10 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style D10 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style E10 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style F10 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style G10 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style H10 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style I10 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style J10 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Programming Framework: A Universal Process Visualization Methodology
sources.notes: Higher-level biological process visualization: Molting Process. Created using the Programming Framework methodology for organismal and ecological process analysis.

sources.title: Molecular Mechanisms of Ecdysone Action: A Gateway to Understanding Arthropod Development
sources.authors: Rewitz, K.F., O'Connor, M.B., Gilbert, L.I.

sources.title: Evolution of Molting and Metamorphosis in Insects

notes: Higher-level biological process. GLMP covers biochemical/molecular processes; this database covers organismal/ecological processes.
